title: Rig Veda Sanhita Anks, 37-38
gloss: The text of the Rig Veda, with an independent commentary in Hindi. Compiled by Rai Bahadur Shiv Nath.
notes: Rig Veda Samhita (Sanskrit text + Hindi commentary), installments 37-38, compiled by a Rai Bahadur — the monumental Arya-Samaj Vedic-publication project (the Veda as the ultimate 'arsh'/revealed authority for Dayananda's movement), serialized. verso index: '47.5-7 Rigveda Samhita 37-40'. Bracket note: previous number at entry No. 17, page 45 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending 31st December 1909 (another 'December 1909' back-reference). Composite pagination 2 and 1602-1696.

title: Rig Veda Sanhita Anks 39-40
notes: Dash-continuation of s5; Rig Veda installments 39-40 (pp. 1697-1792) — the Veda edition rolling past 1700 printed pages, a vast undertaking.

title: Rig Veda Sanhita Anks 7-8
notes: Dash-continuation of s6; earlier Rig Veda installments 7-8 (pp. 241-320) in a 2nd edition — back-numbers reprinted, showing sustained subscriber demand. Closes the Arya-Samaj Vedic-scripture cluster.

author: Hoshiar Singh
title: Mahajani Darpan
gloss: Or Book-keeping and Arithmetic according to native methods.
copyright: Lala Jumman Lal, Hoshiar Singh, Delhi; No. 38, dated the 21st March, 1910.
notes: 'Mirror of Mahajani' — book-keeping and arithmetic in the MAHAJANI script (the merchant/banker shorthand script of the bazaar), paired with Urdu; the persistence of an indigenous commercial script/accounting system against colonial standardization (cf. Desi Hisab p43 s1). A rare Mahajani-script entry; directly documents a vernacular knowledge-system resisting homogenization. '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

title: Karima Farsi ahe Sindi Tarjama San
gloss: The well-known work entitled Karima of Shaikh Sa'di, with its translation in Sindi verses.
notes: Sa'di's 'Karima' (the Pand-nama, a maktab primer-classic) — Persian text with Sindhi verse translation, via Pokhar Das of Shikarpur again; the Sa'di curriculum reaching the Sindhi market (cf. Bostan-i-Sindhi p22; Sa'di's pervasiveness across all this year's languages). The Persian-classics-as-shared-curriculum thread.

author: Fateh Muhammad
title: Takhmis-i-Pand Nama
gloss: Or the Karima, a moral poem in Persian by Sa'di, explained in Urdu verse by addition of three lines more to the original couplets.
notes: A takhmis (five-line expansion) of Sa'di's Pand-nama/Karima — the SAME Persian classic as s1 above but here expanded into Urdu verse; a vivid side-by-side of the two vernacular receptions (Sindhi translation vs Urdu takhmis) of one Sa'di text on one page. The takhmis form itself is a mode of vernacular appropriation of the Persian master-text.

section: BILINGUAL (PUNJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER AND URDU)—MISCELLANEOUS.
author: Chiranji Lal, Lala
title: Fann-i-Tamashbini
gloss: A little book containing verses condemning prostitution and relating the story of a rake who was ruined thereby.
notes: Anti-prostitution moral verse — Punjabi (in Persian/Shahmukhi character) with Urdu, 2000 copies free; the social-purity/moral-reform campaign against the courtesan culture (which is also the tawaif musical culture — a music-thread adjacency: the reformist attack on the courtesan performing tradition). By a Hindu author (Chiranji Lal). On-theme for both moral-reform and the music/performance-culture displacement.
